source: Claims against Malayan Government. Paskoe's Ltd.__CO273:670:4
filename: Claims against Malayan Government. Paskoe's Ltd.__CO273:670:4.txt
summary:
- **Core topic/purpose:** Colonial Office file on **claims by Messrs. Paskoe’s Ltd. (Singapore)** against **Malayan governmental authorities**, chiefly a claim against the **Malayan Broadcasting Corporation (MBC)** for contract work, and how/when to make any payment during wartime when local records were unavailable.  
- **Geography/time period:** Concerns **Singapore/Malaya** during and after the **fall of Singapore (Feb 1942)**; correspondence and decisions mainly **1942–1943** (file marked “Subsequent 1944–46” but content here is 1942–43).  
- **Contract/work described:** Contract work (conversion/adaptation) at the **Cathay Building, Singapore** for MBC; work was **partly completed** at capitulation, creating uncertainty about the final amount properly due.  
- **Claim amounts & payments discussed:** Contract total stated as **S.S.$79,600** (Reid). Payments acknowledged: **S.S.$15,000** (paid about **1 Feb 1942**) and **S.S.$20,000** (instructions/payment into bank about **9 Feb 1942**). A **third certificate** for **S.S.$20,000–$30,000** was reportedly passed for payment but **evidence of payment was lacking**; estimated unfinished work about **S.S.$20,000**, leaving an estimated balance around **S.S.$24,000** (Reid).  
- **Major decision/proposal:** Colonial Office concluded **some further payment was due** but, due to uncertainties (completion status, records, prior payments), decided on a **further part payment of S.S.$10,000** and to **register the remainder** for consideration **after re-occupation of Malaya**; this position was reiterated in late **Sept–Nov 1943** correspondence.  
- **Key actors/organizations:** **Colonial Office** officials (notably **J. J. Paskin**, **B. V. Foster Hall**, **Mr. Duncan**, **Mr. Caine**); **W. R. Reid** (Chief Executive Officer, MBC); **Dale Cuthbertson** (architect/official involved with certificates); solicitors **White & Leonard & Nicholls & Co.** (London) and **Arthur Robinson & Co.** (Melbourne); **Chartered Bank of India, Australia & China**; **High Commissioner (UK) Canberra**; **Trading with the Enemy Department (TWE)** and **Custodian of Enemy Property**.  
- **Bank/priority & legal-policy complications:** Chartered Bank asserted security over Paskoe’s assets via a **registered debenture for S.S.$130,000** and an overdraft of about **S.S.$104,045.33 (31 Jan 1942)**, requesting any payments be made to its **London head office**; Colonial Office sought guidance from **TWE** on whether/how to pay a **company incorporated in Japanese-occupied territory**, and required a **letter of indemnity with sureties** before releasing funds (exchange rate noted **$1 = 2/4d**).
